rulings.Case Title: MHYF HOLDINGS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. CHRIS V. KELLY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 30, 2024 line 1. 2-CROSS DEFENDANT CHRIS KELLY INDIV AND AS CO-TRUSTEE OF THE KELLY FAMILY TRUST, LARA KELLY's DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. As an initial matter the Demurrer to Neill Bassi's Cross-Complaint is sustained with leave to amend to allege standing to bring all/any causes of action as a signatory to the agreement or a third-party beneficiary. Demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to the cause of action for breach of contract for the Cross-Complaint(s) to to allege facts (i) in support of "the breach of contract that occurred when the Kellys filed their cross-complaint attains Broker," (ii) and/or facts supporting the claim that Kellys "fail[ed] to indemnify, defend, and hold Broker harmless' by identifying the claims for which there was failure to (1) indemnify; (2) defend; and/or (3) hold harmless; and/or facts showing that there was a pending or threatened proceeding during the relevant times per Listing Agreement paragraph 6(iv). Demurrer is OVERRULED as to the Express Indemnity and Declaratory Relief causes of action as the indemnity clause is susceptible to interpretation urged by Cross-Complainant. (Zalkind v. Ceradyne, Inc. (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 1010, 1025.) Demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to the cause of action for implied indemnity for the Cross-Complainant to allege facts supporting the claim that there can be any liability stemming from the Buyers' operative complaint against the Brokers, i.e. identify the claims in the complaint asserted against the Broker for which "a judgment be rendered against Broker for damages allegedly sustained by Buyers." (Cross-Complaint, par. 41.) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
